title: Upon Much-More. Epig.
content:
Much-more provides and hoards up like an ant,
    Yet Much-more still complains he is in want.
    Let Much-more justly pay his tithes; then try
    How both his meal and oil will multiply.
meaning:
Robert Herrick's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Upon Much-More. Epig."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
